items.correct: It can come from both ir (to go) and ser (to be); context tells you which - 'Fui a España' means 'I went to Spain'.
items.misconception: Fui only ever comes from ser, so 'Fui a España' must mean 'I was Spain'.
items.random_wrong: Fui is the future tense of tener.
items.why: Ir and ser share identical preterite forms, a notorious overlap; travel narratives almost always use fui as 'I went'.

items.q: Should nationalities be capitalised in Spanish as in English?
items.correct: No - Spanish writes nationalities and languages in lowercase: 'Es francesa y habla inglés'.
items.misconception: Nationalities take a capital letter, as in English.
items.random_wrong: Nationalities are always written entirely in capitals.
items.why: Transferring English capitalisation is a common written error; only country names like Francia keep the capital.

items.correct: Long - 'un viaje largo' is a long journey.
items.misconception: Large or big, because of the spelling.
items.why: 'Largo' is a false friend: 'big' is 'grande', and the mix-up regularly garbles descriptions of journeys and distances.

items.q: 'Un billete de ida y vuelta' is what kind of ticket?
items.why: Correct answer: A return ticket. “A single ticket” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In 'Fui a México el verano pasado', which tense is 'fui'?
items.correct: The preterite, for a completed past action
items.why: The answer examiners want is: The preterite, for a completed past action. “The present tense” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
Correct answer: Climate change. “A change of clothes” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which sentence uses the nationality correctly?
items.why: It's Ella es francesa. “Ella es francés” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
